input.counterSource: None found. All checked sources (MDN, caniuse, Chrome DevRel blog, WebKit blog, CSS-Tricks 2026) consistently confirm masonry remains experimental with no cross-browser stable support.
input.evidence: The MDN source directly states masonry layout has "Limited availability" and is "not Baseline because it does not work in some of the most widely-used browsers," matching the claim verbatim. The MDN page also explicitly labels it "experimental technology" and advises checking the compatibility table before production use. Independent corroboration: caniuse.com reports ~0% global usage with only Firefox 155+ showing stable support for `grid-template-rows: masonry`; Chrome/Edge 140 have it behind a flag; Safari stable does not yet ship it (only Safari 26 / Technology Preview does). A 2026 search confirms no major stable browser channel offers fully interoperable, unflagged support and the syntax is still evolving (earlier `masonry` track value vs. newer `display: grid-lanes` proposals are not yet aligned). The claim is current as of July 2026, the source is a primary authoritative reference (MDN), and the "should not be used in production without a fallback" recommendation is a direct, warranted inference from the documented experimental/limited-availability status — not an overreach. No credible source contradicts it.
